reasoning: The Indeed Job Postings Index has been in a steady decline through 2025 and early 2026, falling from ~111.5 in Jan 2025 to ~101.8 in early April 2026 as the post-pandemic labor market cools. Expert commentary from the Indeed Hiring Lab (Feb/Mar 2026) confirms a cooler labor market with slowed wage growth and flat-to-declining overall postings. Given the negative short-term drift, near-term probabilities (1 week to 3 months) lean comfortably towards a continued slow decline or hovering just below current levels, which sit near the strong pre-pandemic baseline of 100. As time progresses (6 months to 1 year out), the likelihood of stabilization or a 'measured recovery' (as predicted by some economists) starts to slightly raise the probability of being above the April 2026 baseline. For distant horizons (3 to 10 years), extreme uncertainty dominates: we cannot reliably project Indeed's market share, macro business cycles, or the advent of future recessions/expansions against the cyclical peak baseline of Feb 2020, perfectly aligning with the instruction to revert long-term probabilities to ~0.50.
